papers.title: Microstructural Evolution and Mechanical Properties of Nb-Containing Medium-Mn Low-Density Steels
papers.paper_scope: Investigate nano-scale precipitate evolution and mechanical properties of Fe-12Mn-9Al-3Cr-1.4C-(0.02/0.04)Nb medium-Mn low-density steels, focusing on NbC and kappa-carbide precipitation, austenite grain refinement, and strengthening mechanisms.

alloys.alloys_notes: Measured composition (wt%) from Table 1: Mn 12.80, Al 9.01, Cr 3.01, C 1.39, Nb 0.021, Fe Bal.
Measured composition (wt%) from Table 1: Mn 12.82, Al 9.12, Cr 2.99, C 1.40, Nb 0.039, Fe Bal.

processes.description: As-cast condition after centrifugal casting under near-rapid solidification.
processes.processes_notes: Ingot prepared by induction melting in a water-cooled copper crucible, re-melted and poured into a rotating copper mold; strip dimensions 60 mm × 70 mm × 2.5 mm.

structures.overall_structure: Dual-phase microstructure consisting of irregular-shaped ferrite dispersed in an austenite matrix with NbC precipitates.

precipitates.distribution: submicron white particles within austenite matrix

structures.overall_structure: Dual-phase microstructure consisting of irregular-shaped ferrite dispersed in an austenite matrix with NbC precipitates.

precipitates.distribution: submicron white particles within austenite matrix; size and volume fraction slightly increased compared to as-cast
intragranular; no coarsened intergranular kappa-carbides detected

intragranular; consistently smaller than in 0.02Nb steel

characterization_methods.technique: XRD, SEM, EBSD, EPMA, TEM, Vickers hardness, tensile testing
characterization_methods.purpose: Phase identification, microstructural characterization, nano-precipitate analysis, elemental distribution, and mechanical property evaluation
characterization_methods.key_findings: Ferrite (≈16.2 vol%) + austenite dual phase; NbC particles identified by TEM SAED and EDS; intragranular kappa-carbides present.

computational_details.simulation_method: Spin-polarized density functional theory (DFT) within the generalized gradient approximation (GGA) using the Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof (PBE) functional
computational_details.potential_model: Projector augmented-wave (PAW) potentials; plane-wave kinetic energy cutoff 500 eV; Gaussian smearing width 0.05 eV; Hubbard U scheme with U = 2.59 eV for Fe atoms; vacuum spacing 15 Å; Brillouin zone integration with 6×6×6 Monkhorst-Pack k-point mesh; geometry optimization convergence criterion 0.03 eV Å⁻¹

unmapped_findings: The yield strength of 0.04Nb steels exceeds that of 0.02Nb steels by approximately 71 MPa, mainly attributed to additional fine grain strengthening and dislocation strengthening, despite a slight reduction in precipitation strengthening of kappa-carbides with higher Nb content.
Quantitative strengthening mechanism analysis is discussed, but detailed absolute strength contributions for each mechanism are not explicitly tabulated in the provided text.
The austenite grain size of 0.04Nb steels is approximately half that of 0.02Nb steels due to NbC precipitation; absolute grain size values are not provided in the text.

The study mentions investigation of structure evolution and mechanical properties under different aging periods, but specific data beyond the 3 h aging treatment are not included in the provided text.
Absolute Vickers microhardness values, yield strength, ultimate tensile strength, and elongation values were measured but not explicitly reported in the provided text segment.
Exact NbC particle sizes, kappa-carbide sizes, and quantitative volume fraction numerical values are referenced in Figure 4 but not stated in the provided text.
